mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of 2019-07-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 2-month period signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 persons month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and forward-looking economic pessimism, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) relative to single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reduce cyclical equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,023.82 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2019-04-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following strong GDP growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3%, signaling real monetary accommodation that supports equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.161 and FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of 2019-07-01
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) relative to single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand-side shock
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following sharp HOUST declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.
